mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of rate-hiking periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 5-year backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side constraints and factory slowdowns, causing short-term panic selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week during economic contraction phases.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns exceed 50% of triggered instances during NBER-defined recessions, invalidating the short-term panic mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 3.0 percent, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 250 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 3-year windows, or fails to predict quarterly returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic strength that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing a consistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 4-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of GDP expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in industrial production above a 2.0 percent quarterly growth rate signals overheating supply capacity that attracts short-term momentum flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where industrial production growth exceeds the 2.0 percent threshold.
